evidence_spans.span: Maximum-Likelihood Estimation estimates an econometric model by maximizing the likelihood of the observed sample.
MLE is used to fit a probabilistic model by maximizing its likelihood function on observed training data.

rationale: Both mentions refer to the same statistical method (Maximum Likelihood Estimation) with the same core definition: maximizing a likelihood function to estimate model parameters. 'MLE' is a standard abbreviation for 'Maximum-Likelihood Estimation'. The slight difference in domain (econometrics vs. machine learning) does not change the identity of the method.
